disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden, tearing chest pain that was maximal at the very moment of onset
trigger_question_examples: When did the pain start and how quickly did it get bad?
What does the pain feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on all at once, worst right from the first second — a tearing, ripping feeling deep in my chest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain radiates straight through to the interscapular region of the back and is unchanged by movement or breathing
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain go anywhere else, like your back?
Does anything make it better or worse — moving, breathing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It goes straight through to between my shoulder blades, and nothing changes it — moving or breathing makes no difference.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels profoundly lightheaded, nauseous and near-fainting (proxy for haemodynamic compromise)
trigger_question_examples: Do you feel faint or dizzy?
Any other symptoms with the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really lightheaded and sick in the stomach, sweaty, and I feel like I might pass out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known lifelong connective-tissue features — very tall with long limbs/fingers, a previously dislocated eye lens, and a doctor-noted enlargement of the main vessel off the heart
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions or a family/genetic condition?
Has a doctor ever said anything about your heart or main blood vessels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm very tall and super flexible, one of my eye lenses slipped years ago, and a doctor said the big blood vessel off my heart is a bit enlarged and gave me a tablet for it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Poor adherence to the prescribed daily beta-blocker
trigger_question_examples: Are you taking the tablet the doctor prescribed?
How often do you take your heart tablet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I don't take it regularly — I forget or skip it a lot.
